title: Smoky Charred American Pork Skillet
description: A charred, chewy, indulgent American dish featuring mixed spice and render-fat, cooked in a molcajete vessel. This recipe is nut-free-friendly and perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together the smoked paprika, brown sugar, kosher salt, black pepper, garlic powder, and onion powder.
Add the spice mixture to the pork cubes and toss to coat evenly.
Heat the lard or vegetable oil in the molcajete vessel over medium-high heat.
Add the pork cubes to the molcajete and sear until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the molcajete to the preheated oven and cook for 20 minutes, or until the pork is tender and charred.
Remove the molcajete from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Serve the smoky charred pork hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F, then rest 3 minutes before serving.
